prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, I, Godric the Scribe, hath been tasked with recording the mystical happenings of the "Q3" season, a time of great import for the mystical SaaS company known as "Flibberflabber Inc." Here are the tales of their fiscal quarter:

In the year of our Lord 2023, the wizards of Flibberflabber Inc. conjured forth a revenue of 12.5 million golden doubloons, a sum that doth rival the riches of the great merchant guilds of old. Their "customer acquisition costs" didst rise like a mighty dragon, breathing fire upon their coffers to the tune of 3.2 million pieces of silver, yet still they managed to vanquish the beast of "churn" with a mighty 95% retention rate, a feat worthy of the greatest knights of chivalry. Meanwhile, their "gross margin" didst expand like a blooming flower, reaching a robust 85% of their total revenue, a true marvel of modern accounting magic. And lo, their "adjusted EBITDA" didst shine like a beacon of profitability, a shining 20% of their total revenue, a testament to the wise stewardship of their CEO, a veritable wizard of the digital realm.
